title: Coryphantha grahamii
taxonomy: Coryphantha grahamii is classified as a species of cactus within the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Coryphantha, which is a group of small to medium-sized cacti. The species is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and is characterized by its small size and unique flower colors. Coryphantha grahamii is further classified as a member of the subgenus Coryphantha, which is distinguished by its small, tubercled stems. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Coryphantha grahamii from other related cactus species.
